title: Fluentd and Fluent Bit Filter Plugin Drop Rate, Selective Discard Detection, and Regex-Misconfiguration Records-Loss Forensics
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.7.1 buffer saturation, UC-13.7.2 output error rates, UC-13.7.3 throughput mismatch, UC-13.7.4 worker crashes, UC-13.7.5 memory buffer utilization forecasts, UC-13.7.6 output flush latency tails, or UC-13.7.9 TLS errors. Does not mutate live collector configuration; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved pipeline changes.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics with ten-to-fifteen minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to platform logging on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace, deploy a widened grep exclude regex on a security-heavy tag, confirm observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics elevates severity for the affected filter_name with non-null recommended_action within two evaluation windows, and verify selective_signal or compliance_gap flags when high-value keys disappear.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After reverting the regex and restoring steady-state drop_rate below inventory expected_max_drop_pct with quiet otel_collector logs, confirm severities return to low and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs.

dataSources: (index=fluentd_self OR index=fluent OR index=observability OR index=platform) sourcetype=fluentd:self_metrics OR sourcetype=fluentd:worker_stats OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd_self OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd with fluentd_input_status_num_records_total and fluentd_output_status_num_records_total; index=fluentbit_metrics sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentbit with fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total style counters; index=otel_collector sourcetype=otel:collector OR sourcetype=otelcol:log OR sourcetype=splunk_otel:log for filterprocessor decisions; lookups fluentd_filter_inventory.csv and pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv; optional CIM Performance host enrichment via tstats
app: Fluentd 1.x with monitor_agent or Prometheus exporter + Fluent Bit monitoring HTTP server + Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector (optional)
spl:
`comment("UC-13.7.7 Fluentd Fluent Bit filter-plugin drop baselines, IO delta drops, OTel filterprocessor; earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd_self (sourcetype=fluentd:self_metrics OR sourcetype=fluentd:worker_stats OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd_self) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,worker,fluentd_worker,"0")))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_id,pipeline,pipeline_name,"pipe_default"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,k8s_ns,namespace,"global"))))
      | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,filter,section,"unknown_filter"))))
      | eval plugin=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,plugin_type,filter_plugin,"filter"))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,filter_id,filter_name,"flt")))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match_tag,route_tag,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,grep_pattern,"")))
      | eval match=trim(toString(coalesce(match,tag_match,"")))
      | eval regex=trim(toString(coalesce(regex,pattern,rule_regex,"")))
      | eval records_in=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_in,filter_records_in,emit_in_total,records_total_in,"0")),10)
      | eval records_out=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_out,filter_records_out,emit_out_total,records_total_out,"0")),10)
      | eval records_drop_metric=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_dropped,dropped_records,filter_drop_records_total,"0")),10)
      | eval records_dropped=if(records_in>0 AND records_out>=0 AND records_in>records_out, records_in-records_out, records_drop_metric)
      | eval lane="fluentd_self_metrics"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=observability OR index=platform) (sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd OR sourcetype=fluentd:prometheus OR sourcetype=prometheus) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,job,worker,"0")))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"fluentd_input_status_num_records_total|fluentd_output_status_num_records_total")
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,kubernetes_pod_name,route,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval val=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value,metric_value,Value,"0")),10)
      | eval is_in=if(match(mn,"input_status_num_records_total"),1,0)
      | stats max(_time) AS _time sum(eval(if(is_in==1,val,0))) AS in_sum sum(eval(if(is_in==0,val,0))) AS out_sum BY host worker_id tag
      | eval pipeline_id="fluentd_io_ledger"
      | eval tenant_id="global"
      | eval filter_name="aggregate_input_minus_output_gap"
      | eval plugin="fluentd_internal_counter"
      | eval plugin_id="io_delta"
      | eval pattern="fluentd_input_status_num_records_total_minus_output"
      | eval match="__all_tags__"
      | eval regex="ledger_diff"
      | eval records_in=in_sum
      | eval records_out=out_sum
      | eval records_dropped=if(in_sum>out_sum, in_sum-out_sum, 0)
      | eval lane="fluentd_prometheus_io_delta"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
    [ search index=fluentbit_metrics (sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentbit) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,'kubernetes.pod.name',pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,thread_id,"0")))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,pipeline_name,"fb_pipeline"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,namespace,k8s_ns,"global"))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | where like(mn,"%filter%") AND (like(mn,"%drop%") OR like(mn,"%discard%"))
      | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,plugin_name,name,"fluentbit_filter"))))
      | eval plugin="fluent_bit_filter"
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,alias,section,"fb_flt")))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,exclude,"")))
      | eval match=trim(toString(coalesce(match,logical_op,"")))
      | eval regex=trim(toString(coalesce(regex,rule,"")))
      | eval records_in=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_in,records_in_total,proc_records,"0")),10)
      | eval records_out=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_out,emit_records,"0")),10)
      | eval records_dropped=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_dropped,drop_records,metric_value,value,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="fluentbit_metrics_filter_drop"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
    [ search index=otel_collector (sourcetype=otel:collector OR sourcetype=otelcol:log OR sourcetype=splunk_otel:log) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"filterprocessor|filter_processor|drop.*telemetry|regexp.*filter|attributes.*exclude")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,service_instance_id,"otel_collector"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,pipeline_component_id,"otel")))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,service_pipeline,"otel_pipeline"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_id,scope,"global"))))
      | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,processor_name,"otel_filterprocessor"))))
      | eval plugin="otel_filter_processor"
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,component,"otel_flt")))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match_pattern,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,include_pattern,"")))
      | eval match=trim(toString(coalesce(match,exclude_match,"")))
      | eval regex=trim(toString(coalesce(regex,regexp_pattern,"")))
      | eval records_in=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_in,accepted_spans,log_records_in,"0")),10)
      | eval records_out=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_out,exported_spans,log_records_out,"0")),10)
      | eval records_dropped=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_dropped,dropped_items,filtered_out,"1")),10)
      | eval lane="otel_filterprocessor_log"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
| eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"undetermined"))))
| eval merge_key=cluster."|".host."|".coalesce(pipeline_id,"na")."|".coalesce(filter_name,"na")."|".coalesce(plugin_id,"na")
| eval records_in=coalesce(records_in,0)
| eval records_out=coalesce(records_out,0)
| eval records_dropped=coalesce(records_dropped,0)
| eval drop_rate=if(records_in>0, round(100*records_dropped/nullif(records_in,0),4), if(records_dropped>0,100,0))
| sort 0 merge_key - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=f global=f last(drop_rate) AS prev_drop_rate BY merge_key
| eval drop_roc=if(isnotnull(prev_drop_rate), round(drop_rate-prev_drop_rate,4), 0)
| eventstats median(drop_rate) AS baseline_drop_rate stdev(drop_rate) AS baseline_drop_stdev BY filter_name host
| eventstats perc90(drop_rate) AS p90_drop_rate BY filter_name cluster
| eval spike_ratio=if(baseline_drop_rate>0.0001, round(drop_rate/nullif(baseline_drop_rate,0),3), if(drop_rate>0.05,999,0))
| eval selective_signal=if(match(tag,"security|winlog|linux_audit") AND drop_rate>1 AND match(lower(pattern.regex.match),"eventcode|signature|process_name|event_id"),1,0)
| eventstats perc95(drop_rate) AS cluster_drop_p95 BY cluster
| eval unfair_tenant=if(drop_rate>0 AND cluster_drop_p95>0 AND drop_rate>(cluster_drop_p95*2.5) AND tenant_id!="global",1,0)
| join type=left max=0 filter_name
    [| inputlookup fluentd_filter_inventory.csv
     | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,plugin_key,filter_key,""))))
     | fields filter_name expected_max_drop_pct owner_team filter_owner notes ]
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| inputlookup pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_key,""))))
     | eval compliance_sig_fields=required_signature_fields
     | fields tenant_id compliance_sig_fields risk_tier compliance_notes ]
| eval expected_max_drop_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_max_drop_pct,"2.5")),10)
| eval compliance_gap=if(selective_signal==1 OR (len(compliance_sig_fields)>4 AND drop_rate>expected_max_drop_pct),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS host ]
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(drop_rate) AS drop_rate max(baseline_drop_rate) AS baseline_drop_rate max(spike_ratio) AS spike_ratio max(drop_roc) AS drop_roc max(p90_drop_rate) AS p90_drop_rate sum(records_in) AS records_in sum(records_out) AS records_out sum(records_dropped) AS records_dropped max(compliance_gap) AS compliance_gap max(selective_signal) AS selective_signal max(unfair_tenant) AS unfair_tenant first(tag) AS tag first(pattern) AS pattern first(regex) AS regex first(match) AS match first(worker_id) AS worker_id first(plugin) AS plugin first(plugin_id) AS plugin_id first(pipeline_id) AS pipeline_id first(lane) AS lane first(compliance_sig_fields) AS compliance_sig_fields first(risk_tier) AS risk_tier first(notes) AS inventory_notes max(expected_max_drop_pct) AS expected_max_drop_pct max(cpu_load) AS cpu_load BY cluster host tenant_id filter_name
| eval severity=case(compliance_gap==1 AND drop_rate>(expected_max_drop_pct*3),"critical", spike_ratio>=8 AND drop_rate>0.25,"high", spike_ratio>=4 AND drop_rate>0.12,"medium", unfair_tenant==1 AND drop_rate>1,"medium", match(lane,"otel_filterprocessor") AND records_dropped>0 AND drop_roc>0.5,"high", drop_rate>expected_max_drop_pct AND spike_ratio>=3,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(match(severity,"critical|high") AND selective_signal==1,"rollback_regex_and_restore_eventcode_signature_process_name_fields_compare_git_diff", match(severity,"critical|high") AND match(plugin,"fluent_bit"),"inspect_fluentbit_grep_record_modifier_nest_lua_sections_validate_negative_lookahead_and_timeout", match(severity,"critical|high") AND match(plugin,"fluentd_internal_counter"),"reconcile_fluentd_input_output_counters_investigate_unlabeled_output_routes_and_copy_trees", match(severity,"critical|high") AND match(plugin,"otel_filter_processor"),"audit_otel_filterprocessor_include_exclude_lists_validate_regexp_performance_and_sampling", unfair_tenant==1,"slice_drops_by_tenant_id_tag_compare_routing_rules_and_quota_overrides", true(),"refresh_filter_inventory_baselines_and_reschedule_saved_search")
| table last_seen cluster host tenant_id worker_id pipeline_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern regex match records_in records_out records_dropped drop_rate baseline_drop_rate spike_ratio drop_roc compliance_gap unfair_tenant compliance_sig_fields risk_tier severity recommended_action cpu_load
description: Detects rising filter-plugin drop_rate and records_dropped signals across Fluentd self-metrics in index=fluentd_self, coarse ledger gaps from fluentd_input_status_num_records_total minus fluentd_output_status_num_records_total, Fluent Bit filter discard counters in index=fluentbit_metrics, and OpenTelemetry filterprocessor include or exclude telemetry in index=otel_collector, then correlates spike_ratio against per-filter baselines, selective loss of security-relevant keys (EventCode, signature, process_name), per-tenant unfair drops, regex backtracking pressure joined to Performance.cpu_load_percent, and Lua or nest and flatten misconfiguration fingerprints using inventory and compliance lookups.
value: Platform and security governance teams receive defensible evidence when log pipelines silently discard telemetry after regex or sampling misconfiguration, preventing undetected holes in compliance archives and incident reconstruction while giving FinOps and tenant owners a fair-share view of who loses records during shared collector upgrades.
implementation: Land fluentd_self and prometheus fluentd counter scrapes, fluentbit_metrics filter discard series, and otel_collector filterprocessor logs; maintain fluentd_filter_inventory.csv and pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv; schedule observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics every ten to fifteen minutes over earliest=-4h@h; route critical and high severities to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports with worker_id and tag drilldowns.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster, host, and filter_name with drilldowns to fluentd_self JSON, prometheus fluentd counter lines, fluentbit_metrics scrape documents, otel_collector filterprocessor logs, and lookup-enriched owner columns; single-value tiles for compliance_gap counts; timeline of spike_ratio and drop_roc.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS shipper_host
| join type=left max=0 shipper_host
    [| inputlookup fluentd_filter_inventory.csv
     | eval shipper_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shipper_hint,host,""))))
     | fields shipper_host filter_owner expected_max_drop_pct ]
| where cpu_load>0 OR mem_used>0
| table shipper_host cpu_load mem_used filter_owner expected_max_drop_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for hosts and pods running Fluentd, Fluent Bit, or OpenTelemetry Collector when cpu_load_percent contextualizes regex backtracking storms; retain thirty days for quarterly logging attestations.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Docker logs with OpenTelemetry (pipeline context)

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate denylist deploys during approved change windows elevate drop_rate by design until baselines retrain; require inventory expected_max_drop_pct gates and change_ticket correlation before paging. Security incident response teams add aggressive grep excludes to stop bleeding; pair dwell thresholds with spike_ratio so short-lived incident filters do not masquerade as compliance loss. Intentional sampling and cardinality caps on development clusters produce chronic medium severities that production routing should downgrade using environment labels. Planned redaction policies that remove PII keys can trip selective_signal heuristics until pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv documents exempt tenants. Structured-log adoption shifts parsing and key shapes so historical median baselines look like spikes; freeze baselines during migrations or widen training windows. Regex engine upgrades change backtracking behavior without configuration edits; compare vendor release notes before rollback. Duplicate scrapers double-count Prometheus counters and fabricate ledger gaps; dedupe federation targets. OpenTelemetry debug logging left on floods disk and can suppress useful lines; treat collector log volume as its own health signal. Lookup staleness after renames of filter_name or tenant_id produces false compliance_gap rows; refresh CSVs weekly from Git.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode grouping on pipeline_id and tenant_id when filter drops repeat across clusters

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline engineering lead, the logging platform Site Reliability Engineering pod, security operations partners who depend on tamper-evident Windows Event identifiers and endpoint telemetry, and compliance reviewers who map log completeness to retention and attestation programs. This use case isolates intentional or accidental record removal inside Fluentd and Fluent Bit filter plugins and OpenTelemetry Collector filterprocessor stages—not buffer saturation (UC-13.7.1), not output error rates (UC-13.7.2), not ingress versus egress throughput imbalance (UC-13.7.3), not worker crash loops (UC-13.7.4), not memory buffer fill forecasting (UC-13.7.5), not output flush latency tails (UC-13.7.6), and not TLS handshake failures on forwarders (UC-13.7.9). When those siblings fire, you may still have healthy filters; when this UC fires, the pipeline is mathematically or semantically eating records before they reach your evidence store.

Fluentd exposes per-plugin counters through worker-local monitor agents, Prometheus scrape targets, or structured self-metrics indexes you designate as index=fluentd_self. Practitioners flatten worker_id, tag, plugin_id, filter_name, plugin, pattern, match, regex, records_in, records_out, and records_dropped fields so Splunk can baseline drop_rate per filter. The grep, record_transformer, record_modifier, parser, kubernetes, nest, and lua filter families each admit configurations that drop silently: grep exclude blocks, record_transformer remove_keys, record_modifier remove_keys, parser reserve_data false patterns, kubernetes metadata filters paired with aggressive label selectors, nest and flatten mis-keying that yields empty records, and lua rescue paths that swallow exceptions and return nil. Regex changes merged from continuous integration without canary traffic routinely spike drop_rate when a character class or negative lookahead widens unexpectedly.

Fluent Bit publishes filter discard counters on its monitoring HTTP interface; land JSON scrapes in index=fluentbit_metrics with sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics or prometheus-compatible lines. fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total style series name variants should be normalized at ingest. Per-tenant unfairness appears when one tenant_id or kubernetes namespace label sees disproportionate drops versus cluster peers sharing the same ConfigMap revision.

OpenTelemetry Collector filterprocessor configurations—often shipped with Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector distributions—log include and exclude decisions when debug logging is enabled; index those lines in index=otel_collector alongside structured component health events. Regular expression performance issues surface as engine timeouts or backtracking storms that correlate with elevated CPU on the collector host; join Splunk CIM Performance.cpu_load_percent via tstats to separate CPU-bound regex from benign discard policy.

RBAC and privacy: metric scrapers and fluentd_self forwarders must use dedicated service accounts. Pager templates should quote pattern and regex fragments cautiously because they may embed substrings from live security telemetry. Legal review should confirm whether tenant_id values may appear in evidence exports.

Index design: retain at least fourteen days hot for filter regression replay and thirty days for forensic review after major collector upgrades. Maintain fluentd_filter_inventory.csv with columns filter_name, expected_max_drop_pct, owner_team, filter_owner, and notes describing approved sampling or denylist rationale. Maintain pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv with tenant_id, required_signature_fields, risk_tier, and compliance_notes listing high-value keys such as EventCode, signature, and process_name that must survive the filter chain.

Risk briefing: approved denylist deployments and intentional sampling will move drop_rate by design; inventory lookups and change_ticket_id metadata must gate severity. Security teams legitimately add grep excludes during incidents; require dwell time and spike_ratio multiples before paging application owners. Structured log adoption can change cardinality and make historical baselines look like spikes until baselines retrain.

Differentiation recap: filter-stage discard rate baselines, selective high-value field loss, aggregate Fluentd input minus output ledger gaps, Fluent Bit filter drop counters, OpenTelemetry filterprocessor forensics, per-tenant unfair drop hints, and regex or Lua failure correlation—not buffer queues, not destination HTTP errors, not TLS alone.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary multisearch arms plus lookup tables so a silent outage in one telemetry lane does not hide a genuine discard regression.

Fluentd self-metrics arm: poll worker-local HTTP surfaces or ship Prometheus text from exporters that emit per-filter records_in and records_out. Land events in index=fluentd_self with sourcetype=fluentd:self_metrics or sourcetype=fluentd:worker_stats. Include pipeline_id, worker_id, tag, filter_name, plugin_id, plugin, pattern, match, regex, and explicit records_dropped when the exporter provides it. Stamp cluster, kubernetes_cluster, or k8s_cluster_name consistently for fleet rollups.

Fluentd internal counter arm: scrape fluentd_input_status_num_records_total and fluentd_output_status_num_records_total from the same Prometheus jobs that already monitor Fluentd when enabled. The difference between summed inputs and summed outputs across aligned labels is a coarse dropped-record proxy for mis-routed copy trees, unlabeled outputs, or filters that never emit. Store in index=fluent or index=observability with sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd. Preserve host and worker_id label dimensions.

Fluent Bit metrics arm: enable HTTP monitoring per Fluent Bit administration guidance and scrape /api/v1/metrics into index=fluentbit_metrics. Map fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total and related filter discard counters with plugin_id and pipeline sections. Retain kubernetes.pod.name or pod_name for DaemonSet drilldowns.

OpenTelemetry Collector arm: forward collector logs at info or debug when filterprocessor decision tracing is enabled into index=otel_collector with sourcetype=otel:collector or sourcetype=otelcol:log. Include component, pipeline, include and exclude rule identifiers, and error text for regexp compile or Lua extension failures when present.

Lookup governance: curate fluentd_filter_inventory.csv weekly from Git repositories that own Fluentd and Fluent Bit configuration. Curate pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv with security architecture so the platform knows which signature fields must not disappear behind record_transformer remove_keys.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE generously for wide Prometheus lines, TIME_FORMAT for RFC3339 scrapes, and FIELDALIAS maps from dotted Kubernetes labels to host when needed.

Pre-save validation: confirm index=fluentd_self returns non-zero filter events within two poll intervals after deploy; confirm fluentbit_metrics moves when you intentionally exclude a canary namespace in lab; confirm otel_collector shows filterprocessor lines when debug is toggled under change control.

Example operator helpers:

```bash
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:24220/api/plugins.json | head
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:2020/api/v1/metrics | rg -i filter
kubectl -n observability logs deploy/splunk-otel-collector --tail=200 | rg -i filter
```

Dashboards: heatmap drop_rate by filter_name and cluster, timeline spike_ratio, and compliance_gap indicators keyed to tenant_id.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics with a ten-to-fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate cluster, host, filter_name rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, lane, spike_ratio, and compliance_gap in pager text so incident commanders open filter runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans fluentd_self structured filter counters, Fluentd Prometheus input versus output ledger math, fluentbit_metrics filter discard counters, and otel_collector filterprocessor diagnostics. coalesce ladders unify host, worker_id, and tag naming drift. streamstats measures short-window drop_roc velocity for sudden continuous-integration regex regressions. eventstats builds median baseline_drop_rate per filter_name and host plus perc90 cluster peers. inputlookup fluentd_filter_inventory.csv attaches expected_max_drop_pct and ownership. inputlookup pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv attaches compliance_sig_fields (sourced from the CSV required_signature_fields column) for selective_signal heuristics. join with tstats enriches Performance.cpu_load_percent to correlate catastrophic backtracking with CPU saturation. case maps combined compliance_gap, spike_ratio, unfair_tenant hints, and absolute drop_rate against inventory expectations into severity tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.7 Fluentd Fluent Bit filter-plugin drop baselines, IO delta drops, OTel filterprocessor; earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd_self (sourcetype=fluentd:self_metrics OR sourcetype=fluentd:worker_stats OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd_self) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,worker,fluentd_worker,"0")))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_id,pipeline,pipeline_name,"pipe_default"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,k8s_ns,namespace,"global"))))
      | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,filter,section,"unknown_filter"))))
      | eval plugin=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,plugin_type,filter_plugin,"filter"))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,filter_id,filter_name,"flt")))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match_tag,route_tag,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,grep_pattern,"")))
      | eval match=trim(toString(coalesce(match,tag_match,"")))
      | eval regex=trim(toString(coalesce(regex,pattern,rule_regex,"")))
      | eval records_in=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_in,filter_records_in,emit_in_total,records_total_in,"0")),10)
      | eval records_out=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_out,filter_records_out,emit_out_total,records_total_out,"0")),10)
      | eval records_drop_metric=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_dropped,dropped_records,filter_drop_records_total,"0")),10)
      | eval records_dropped=if(records_in>0 AND records_out>=0 AND records_in>records_out, records_in-records_out, records_drop_metric)
      | eval lane="fluentd_self_metrics"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
    [ search (index=fluent OR index=observability OR index=platform) (sourcetype=prometheus:fluentd OR sourcetype=fluentd:prometheus OR sourcetype=prometheus) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,job,worker,"0")))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"fluentd_input_status_num_records_total|fluentd_output_status_num_records_total")
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,kubernetes_pod_name,route,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval val=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value,metric_value,Value,"0")),10)
      | eval is_in=if(match(mn,"input_status_num_records_total"),1,0)
      | stats max(_time) AS _time sum(eval(if(is_in==1,val,0))) AS in_sum sum(eval(if(is_in==0,val,0))) AS out_sum BY host worker_id tag
      | eval pipeline_id="fluentd_io_ledger"
      | eval tenant_id="global"
      | eval filter_name="aggregate_input_minus_output_gap"
      | eval plugin="fluentd_internal_counter"
      | eval plugin_id="io_delta"
      | eval pattern="fluentd_input_status_num_records_total_minus_output"
      | eval match="__all_tags__"
      | eval regex="ledger_diff"
      | eval records_in=in_sum
      | eval records_out=out_sum
      | eval records_dropped=if(in_sum>out_sum, in_sum-out_sum, 0)
      | eval lane="fluentd_prometheus_io_delta"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
    [ search index=fluentbit_metrics (sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus:fluentbit) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,'kubernetes.pod.name',pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,thread_id,"0")))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,pipeline_name,"fb_pipeline"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,namespace,k8s_ns,"global"))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | where like(mn,"%filter%") AND (like(mn,"%drop%") OR like(mn,"%discard%"))
      | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,plugin_name,name,"fluentbit_filter"))))
      | eval plugin="fluent_bit_filter"
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,alias,section,"fb_flt")))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,exclude,"")))
      | eval match=trim(toString(coalesce(match,logical_op,"")))
      | eval regex=trim(toString(coalesce(regex,rule,"")))
      | eval records_in=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_in,records_in_total,proc_records,"0")),10)
      | eval records_out=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_out,emit_records,"0")),10)
      | eval records_dropped=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_dropped,drop_records,metric_value,value,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="fluentbit_metrics_filter_drop"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
    [ search index=otel_collector (sourcetype=otel:collector OR sourcetype=otelcol:log OR sourcetype=splunk_otel:log) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"filterprocessor|filter_processor|drop.*telemetry|regexp.*filter|attributes.*exclude")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,service_instance_id,"otel_collector"))))
      | eval worker_id=trim(toString(coalesce(worker_id,pipeline_component_id,"otel")))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline,service_pipeline,"otel_pipeline"))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_id,scope,"global"))))
      | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,processor_name,"otel_filterprocessor"))))
      | eval plugin="otel_filter_processor"
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,component,"otel_flt")))
      | eval tag=trim(toString(coalesce(tag,match_pattern,"__all_tags__")))
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,include_pattern,"")))
      | eval match=trim(toString(coalesce(match,exclude_match,"")))
      | eval regex=trim(toString(coalesce(regex,regexp_pattern,"")))
      | eval records_in=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_in,accepted_spans,log_records_in,"0")),10)
      | eval records_out=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_out,exported_spans,log_records_out,"0")),10)
      | eval records_dropped=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(records_dropped,dropped_items,filtered_out,"1")),10)
      | eval lane="otel_filterprocessor_log"
      | fields _time host worker_id pipeline_id tenant_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern match regex records_in records_out records_dropped lane ]
| eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,kubernetes_cluster,k8s_cluster_name,"undetermined"))))
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",host,"|",coalesce(pipeline_id,"na"),"|",coalesce(filter_name,"na"),"|",coalesce(plugin_id,"na"))
| eval records_in=coalesce(records_in,0)
| eval records_out=coalesce(records_out,0)
| eval records_dropped=coalesce(records_dropped,0)
| eval drop_rate=if(records_in>0, round(100*records_dropped/nullif(records_in,0),4), if(records_dropped>0,100,0))
| sort 0 merge_key - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=f global=f last(drop_rate) AS prev_drop_rate BY merge_key
| eval drop_roc=if(isnotnull(prev_drop_rate), round(drop_rate-prev_drop_rate,4), 0)
| eventstats median(drop_rate) AS baseline_drop_rate stdev(drop_rate) AS baseline_drop_stdev BY filter_name host
| eventstats perc90(drop_rate) AS p90_drop_rate BY filter_name cluster
| eval spike_ratio=if(baseline_drop_rate>0.0001, round(drop_rate/nullif(baseline_drop_rate,0),3), if(drop_rate>0.05,999,0))
| eval selective_signal=if(match(tag,"security|winlog|linux_audit") AND drop_rate>1 AND match(lower(strcat(pattern,regex,match)),"eventcode|signature|process_name|event_id"),1,0)
| eventstats perc95(drop_rate) AS cluster_drop_p95 BY cluster
| eval unfair_tenant=if(drop_rate>0 AND cluster_drop_p95>0 AND drop_rate>(cluster_drop_p95*2.5) AND tenant_id!="global",1,0)
| join type=left max=0 filter_name
    [| inputlookup fluentd_filter_inventory.csv
     | eval filter_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(filter_name,plugin_key,filter_key,""))))
     | fields filter_name expected_max_drop_pct owner_team filter_owner notes ]
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| inputlookup pipeline_compliance_required_signatures.csv
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_key,""))))
     | eval compliance_sig_fields=required_signature_fields
     | fields tenant_id compliance_sig_fields risk_tier compliance_notes ]
| eval expected_max_drop_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_max_drop_pct,"2.5")),10)
| eval compliance_gap=if(selective_signal==1 OR (len(compliance_sig_fields)>4 AND drop_rate>expected_max_drop_pct),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS host ]
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen max(drop_rate) AS drop_rate max(baseline_drop_rate) AS baseline_drop_rate max(spike_ratio) AS spike_ratio max(drop_roc) AS drop_roc max(p90_drop_rate) AS p90_drop_rate sum(records_in) AS records_in sum(records_out) AS records_out sum(records_dropped) AS records_dropped max(compliance_gap) AS compliance_gap max(selective_signal) AS selective_signal max(unfair_tenant) AS unfair_tenant first(tag) AS tag first(pattern) AS pattern first(regex) AS regex first(match) AS match first(worker_id) AS worker_id first(plugin) AS plugin first(plugin_id) AS plugin_id first(pipeline_id) AS pipeline_id first(lane) AS lane first(compliance_sig_fields) AS compliance_sig_fields first(risk_tier) AS risk_tier first(notes) AS inventory_notes max(expected_max_drop_pct) AS expected_max_drop_pct max(cpu_load) AS cpu_load BY cluster host tenant_id filter_name
| eval severity=case(compliance_gap==1 AND drop_rate>(expected_max_drop_pct*3),"critical", spike_ratio>=8 AND drop_rate>0.25,"high", spike_ratio>=4 AND drop_rate>0.12,"medium", unfair_tenant==1 AND drop_rate>1,"medium", match(lane,"otel_filterprocessor") AND records_dropped>0 AND drop_roc>0.5,"high", drop_rate>expected_max_drop_pct AND spike_ratio>=3,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(match(severity,"critical|high") AND selective_signal==1,"rollback_regex_and_restore_eventcode_signature_process_name_fields_compare_git_diff", match(severity,"critical|high") AND match(plugin,"fluent_bit"),"inspect_fluentbit_grep_record_modifier_nest_lua_sections_validate_negative_lookahead_and_timeout", match(severity,"critical|high") AND match(plugin,"fluentd_internal_counter"),"reconcile_fluentd_input_output_counters_investigate_unlabeled_output_routes_and_copy_trees", match(severity,"critical|high") AND match(plugin,"otel_filter_processor"),"audit_otel_filterprocessor_include_exclude_lists_validate_regexp_performance_and_sampling", unfair_tenant==1,"slice_drops_by_tenant_id_tag_compare_routing_rules_and_quota_overrides", true(),"refresh_filter_inventory_baselines_and_reschedule_saved_search")
| table last_seen cluster host tenant_id worker_id pipeline_id filter_name plugin plugin_id tag pattern regex match records_in records_out records_dropped drop_rate baseline_drop_rate spike_ratio drop_roc compliance_gap unfair_tenant compliance_sig_fields risk_tier severity recommended_action cpu_load
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if multisearch queue time exceeds platform budgets, summarize per merge_key ten-minute buckets into a summary index and widen alert windows while retaining this detailed search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth starts on the collector. Compare live Fluentd /api/plugins.json filter sections for records_in versus records_out to index=fluentd_self rows for the same worker_id and plugin_id within one poll interval. When numbers diverge, verify label rewriting, duplicate scrapers, and clock skew before blaming parsers.

Fluent Bit validation: curl /api/v1/metrics and confirm fluentbit_filter_drop_records_total deltas align with Splunk sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics derivatives for the same host.

OpenTelemetry validation: toggle filterprocessor debug under vendor guidance and confirm otel_collector logs cite the same include and exclude identifiers your Git commit introduced.

Synthetic regression: in lab, widen a grep exclude regex intentionally, confirm observability_uc_13_7_7_filter_plugin_drop_forensics elevates severity within two windows, then revert and confirm return to low.

Negative test: on healthy estates with quiet denylist drift, confirm critical and high filters yield zero rows for thirty consecutive evaluations.

Runbook linkage: document intentional sampling windows with change_ticket_id annotations so validators do not file false defects during approved FinOps campaigns.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — grep exclude regex merged from continuous integration removes high-value EventCode fields: revert commit, restore negative lookahead tests, and replay a canary Windows security channel sample through the filter chain before fleet rollout.

Case 2 — record_transformer remove_keys drops signature and process_name on Linux audit records: split transforms so compliance keys remain in a parallel non-discarding branch or route copies to a restricted index before aggressive noise stripping.

Case 3 — record_modifier scrub_redact paired with broad filter match clauses and conditional emit omits entire records: replace drop-on-mismatch with null-field redaction where policy permits so cardinality telemetry still proves continuity.

Case 4 — parser reserve_data false after schema change discards unparsed tail bytes: tighten time_key and types definitions, or route raw unparsed events to a quarantine sourcetype for analyst inspection.

Case 5 — kubernetes filter label_selector mismatch after node pool relabel silently excludes tenant workloads: reconcile labels with infrastructure-as-code and verify tenant_id tag continuity in multisearch arms.

Case 6 — nest or flatten misconfiguration yields empty records that downstream outputs skip: validate key names and wildcard paths against vendor examples, and add unit tests for representative JSON payloads.

Case 7 — lua filter exception path returns nil and drops whole messages: wrap Lua with structured error counters, cap execution time, and fail open to a dead-letter tag when compliance demands no silent loss.

Case 8 — PCRE catastrophic backtracking on a security log line raises CPU and correlates with rising drop_rate: simplify regex, use possessive quantifiers where supported, or relocate expensive matching to search-time analytics instead of edge filters.

Case 9 — OpenTelemetry filterprocessor exclude regexp mirrors Fluent Bit rules but diverges after dual commit skew: enforce single source of truth in Git with exported YAML artifacts referenced by both collectors.

Case 10 — per-tenant unfair drops where one tenant_id spikes while siblings stay calm: inspect quota-based sampling, cardinality protection, and router copy trees that accidentally starve a single namespace.

Case 11 — false calm when fluentd_self scraper fails yet Prometheus still healthy: alert on absent heartbeats per inventory host and reconcile dual feeds before trusting all-clear severity.

Case 12 — aggregate fluentd_input_status_num_records_total minus fluentd_output_status_num_records_total grows without matching per-filter counters: investigate copy trees, secondary outputs, and label cardinality explosions that split metric series.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists fluentd_self, fluentd Prometheus IO delta, fluentbit_metrics, and otel_collector arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table lists more than ten analyst columns including worker_id, tag, pattern, regex, match, records_in, records_out, records_dropped, drop_rate, baseline_drop_rate, spike_ratio, compliance_gap, unfair_tenant, risk_tier, severity, recommended_action, and cpu_load.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the trimming rules on your log forwarders so we notice when lines start vanishing after a bad pattern change, when important identifiers disappear, or when one customer stream loses far more than its neighbors. We help teams fix the configuration before audits or security reviews lose their trail.
hardware: Linux hosts and Kubernetes worker nodes running Fluentd, Fluent Bit DaemonSets, or Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector agents that execute filter chains before Splunk HTTP Event Collector, Kafka, Elasticsearch, or object storage outputs.
